# aterm Foundation and AST Patterns

## aterm Foundation

All expression types are maximally-shared immutable terms built on `atermpp::aterm`. Equality is constant-time (pointer comparison). **This is the most important architectural fact in the codebase** — all AST types (`data::variable`, `data::application`, `lps::linear_process`, etc.) are ultimately `aterm` wrappers.

### Constructing terms

Prefer typed `make_` free functions to construct into an existing variable (avoids temporaries):
```cpp
// Typed make_ function (preferred)
data::variable v;
data::make_variable(v, core::identifier_string("x"), sort_expression);

// Generic term construction
data::variable v;
atermpp::make_term_appl(v, core::detail::function_symbol_DataVarId(), name, sort);

// Direct construction (creates a temporary)
data::variable v(core::identifier_string("x"), sort_expression);

// Function application (apply head to arguments)
data::application app(head_expr, arg1, arg2);

// aterm_list from an initializer list or from iterators
data::variable_list vars{v1, v2, v3};
data::variable_list vars2(my_vector.begin(), my_vector.end());
```

### Casting

Expression types form a hierarchy rooted at `atermpp::aterm`. Use `atermpp::down_cast<T>`, never `static_cast`/`dynamic_cast`. Always check the type first with an `is_` function:
```cpp
if (data::is_variable(expr))
{
  const data::variable& v = atermpp::down_cast<data::variable>(expr);
  // use v.name(), v.sort(), etc.
}
else if (data::is_application(expr))
{
  const data::application& app = atermpp::down_cast<data::application>(expr);
  // use app.head(), app[1], etc.
}

// Accessing sub-terms within a class (common pattern)
const sort_expression& sort() const
{
  return atermpp::down_cast<sort_expression>((*this)[1]);
}
```
`down_cast` is zero-cost in release, asserts validity in debug. Use `vertical_cast` when casting strictly up/down the inheritance chain, `assign_cast` for the non-const assign variant.

## Builder and Traverser Patterns

AST transformation and traversal use CRTP templates:
- **Builder** (`builder.h`): mutable AST transformation — override `apply(result, x)`, `enter(x)`, `leave(x)`.
- **Traverser** (`traverser.h`): read-only AST walk — override `apply(x)`, `enter(x)`, `leave(x)`.

## Code Generation

Many builders, traversers, and overloads are auto-generated by Python scripts in `scripts/code_generation/`. Generated sections are delimited by markers of the form:
```cpp
//--- start generated class variable ---//
...
//--- end generated class variable ---//
```
Hand-written code goes in `//--- start user section ... ---//` blocks. **Never edit between generated markers — regenerate instead.** When modernizing generated code, update the templates in `scripts/code_generation/` so regeneration does not regress.
